source_label: Wikipedia Talk ¬ß Pronunciation (Peter Gustav Lejeune Dirichlet)
notes: Named after German mathematician Peter Gustav Lejeune Dirichlet ‚Äî ubiquitous in ML via the Dirichlet distribution and Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA, Blei/Ng/Jordan 2003). The surname LOOKS French (from 'Richelette' in French-speaking Belgium) but he lived in German lands, so two camps exist: the German-style deer-ick-LAY (hard 'k', /kle…™/) vs the French-style deer-ish-LAY (soft 'sh', / Éle…™/). Final stress on -LAY; the 't' is silent in both common readings (though a minority, citing Calabi, insist on a hard final 't'). Wikipedia's own talk page never resolved it ‚Äî a contributor jokes that saying it 'correctly' makes you sound wrong to most mathematicians. Wikipedia documents the English IPA as the hard-k /Àåd…™…ôr…™Ààkle…™/; many ML practitioners say deer-ish-LAY.
